metadata.description: A host- or cluster-scoped virtual network that workloads (VMs, pods) attach to — the attachment POINT between guest vNICs and the host L2 stack. Realized by e.g. a libvirt network (forward mode=bridge/nat/routed/isolated), a Kubernetes NetworkAttachmentDefinition (Multus/CNI), or an OVN logical switch — the vendor mechanism is the provider, never the type. Completes graph traversal guest → VirtualNetwork → (references) underlying bridge/uplink Hardware.NetworkInterface → bond → NIC → switch. Consumers declare attachment by binds_to on the consumer side (a VM binds_to its VirtualNetwork), which implies depends_on: the network exists before the guest starts; guests stop before it tears down.

context.purpose: Models the attachment point workloads plug into — the host- or cluster-scoped network a guest names when it says attach me here.
context.plain_description: The network a VM's or pod's NIC attaches to: a libvirt network, a Kubernetes NetworkAttachmentDefinition, an OVN logical switch — whichever; the mechanism is the provider. It declares how traffic leaves (bridged straight onto a host bridge, NATed, routed, or isolated) and references downward: the VLAN segment it rides and the host bridge or uplink interface supporting it. That downward reference completes the walkable path guest → virtual network → bridge → bond → NIC → switch.
use_when: You need VMs or pods to attach to networks by selecting an existing named network rather than describing L2 details inline.
You need the guest-to-physical-network path traversable for impact analysis — e.g. which guests a bridge change affects.
not_for: The VLAN id or segment itself — Network.VLAN; a virtual network rides a segment, referenced not restated.
The host bridge device — Hardware.NetworkInterface (device_class bridge) supports this network from below.
Per-guest NIC intent — that lives on Compute.VirtualMachine's own networks list.
works_with: Compute.VirtualMachine / Compute.Cluster — the guests that attach, and the scope that hosts the network.
Network.VLAN — the underlying segment, selected by reference.
Hardware.NetworkInterface — the supporting bridge or uplink on the host.
Network.IPAddressPool — the address pool scoped to this segment.

forward_mode.description: How traffic leaves the virtual network (libvirt forward-mode vocabulary). bridge = transparent onto an underlying host bridge; nat/routed = via the host stack; isolated = no egress.

autostart.description: Network is activated with the host.

location.description: The network's name on its host/cluster (e.g. 'host-bridge', 'default') — unique within the containing scope.

role.description: Semantic usage, e.g. workload | mgmt | test.

attachment_ready.description: Network is active and attachable once Realized (libvirt net-list active analog).

relationships.name: host
relationships.description: The host providing a host-scoped virtual network (libvirt case). Guests stop before the host regardless; this scopes the network's lifecycle to its host.

relationships.name: cluster
relationships.description: Cluster-scoped virtual networks (NetworkAttachmentDefinition / OVN logical networks).

supporting_node (RFC 8345; renamed from underlying_device when relation names were adopted from standards, common-elements §9) — the bridge (or uplink) interface a forward_mode=bridge network forwards onto. The hop that joins the guest-attachment layer to the physical L2 stack (common-elements §7/§7a).
relationships.name: supporting_node
segment
relationships.description: The foundational Network.VLAN segment this virtual network rides (selected by reference, not inlined) — docs/foundational-resources.md.

adopts.role: forward-mode vocabulary (bridge/nat/routed/isolated) and host-scoped network lifecycle
relation vocabulary — supporting_node overlay-to-underlay relation name (common-elements §9)
